title: With the Military Ascendant, Is This the End for Imran Khan?
gist: Pakistan's PPP won nine of 21 Gilgit-Baltistan seats in delayed June 2026 polls that Imran Khan's PTI alleges were rigged by the military establishment, amid systematic obstruction of PTI campaigning. (Text is a truncated teaser ending in '[...]'.)

insights.claim: The Pakistan People's Party (PPP) won nine of 21 seats to emerge victorious in the delayed June 7, 2026 Gilgit-Baltistan polls, with results finalized only on June 19 amid misappropriation allegations from Imran Khan's PTI.
insights.speaker: Kunwar Khuldune Shahid
insights.so_what: PPP gains in a Kashmir-disputed territory reinforce the establishment-aligned political balance and PTI's continued marginalization — a Pakistan political-stability signal.
insights.quote: the Pakistan People's Party (PPP) bagging nine out of 21 seats to emerge victorious

insights.claim: The PTI alleges the Gilgit-Baltistan polls were rigged by the military establishment to sideline the party, mirroring its claims about the 2024 general elections, with eyewitnesses confirming discrepancies and pre-poll boundary and voter-list alterations.
insights.speaker: Kunwar Khuldune Shahid
insights.so_what: Continued military engineering of elections points to entrenched hybrid control in Pakistan — relevant to civil-military and policy-continuity risk.
insights.quote: the Gilgit-Baltistan polls too were rigged by the military establishment to sideline the party
insights.locator: 'The PTI insisted that, like the 2024 general elections'

insights.claim: The PTI is being forced to field independent candidates, with its leaders frequently barred from campaigning, signaling systematic state obstruction of the party.
insights.speaker: Kunwar Khuldune Shahid
insights.so_what: Structural suppression of the country's most popular party deepens Pakistan's legitimacy deficit and political-risk premium.
insights.quote: the PTI is being forced to field independent candidates, with its leaders frequently barred from campaigning
insights.locator: 'On top of that, the PTI is being forced'
